title: crossandra infundibuliformis subsp. crocea
intro: Crossandra infundibuliformis subsp. crocea, also known as the "Golden Firebells," is a flowering plant species native to South Africa and India. It is valued for its vibrant yellow flowers and has been traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, including its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects. The plant is believed to offer various health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues, and supporting immune function. In herbal preparations, Crossandra infundibuliformis subsp. crocea is often made into teas, infusions, or tinctures to leverage its medicinal properties.
